AboutPage.metaDesc: Auto body & paint shop in Uvita, Costa Ballena. Nine years in the trade, controlled booth, written warranty. Meet the founder and crew.
AboutPage.heroTitle: Precision collision and paint in Uvita.
AboutPage.heroLede: Nine years in the trade. Six building this shop. One standard of work.
paragraphs: I am Fabricio Ríos Ortiz, founder of Uvita Body Shop. I entered the industry in 2017 after years of watching my father fix problems with his hands — and I decided mine would be fixing what others wrote off.

In 2020 I opened the shop in Uvita with a simple idea: bring Costa Ballena the finish quality you usually only see in San José. No rushed jobs, no yard paint jobs, no "good enough". If it is not perfect, it does not leave the shop.

Today we handle frame repair, full resprays, touch-ups, dent repair and accessory installs for the entire South Zone — Uvita, Dominical, Ojochal, Bahía Ballena. We run Roberlo, BESA, 3M and VICCO because they are the same systems dealerships use — and our written warranty depends on materials that last.

The shop is mine, but the finish belongs to everyone: the customer who trusts us with the car, the team that preps it, the controlled booth and the infrared oven that cures every coat. We do not patch cars up — we restore them.
AboutPage.ownerImageAlt: Fabricio Ríos Ortiz at work in the Uvita Body Shop booth

Team.title: Hands behind every finish
Team.lede: Five craftsmen who pull, prep, paint and check every car that comes through. Years of trade between them, one standard between us.

carlos.bio: Reads damage by feel before sight. He measures every panel before he pulls it.
carlos.alt: Carlos, body and frame technician at Uvita Body Shop

rafael.bio: Mixes color until it disappears against the original panel. The hardest part of paint is making it look like nothing happened.
rafael.alt: Rafael, paint and color-match specialist at Uvita Body Shop

alfredo.bio: Years in the booth show in every coat that cures clean. Trains the rest of us on what the manual leaves out.
alfredo.alt: Alfredo, senior painter at Uvita Body Shop

luis.bio: Where the finish is decided. Sands, masks, primes — gets the substrate right so the paint can do its job.
luis.alt: Luis, prep and surface technician at Uvita Body Shop

johel.bio: Last set of eyes before the car leaves. Catches what nobody wants to find later.
johel.alt: Johel, detail and final-inspection technician at Uvita Body Shop

LeadCapture.title: Request a quote
LeadCapture.description: Send your name, phone, service, and a short description of the work. We reply during business hours — or message us on WhatsApp from the Contact page.

ContactPage.heroLede: Use the form or message us on WhatsApp. We reply during business hours and schedule a visit or drop-off.

ServicesIndexPage.metaDesc: Auto body & paint services in Uvita: frame repair, full paint, touch-ups, dent repair, and accessory installs across the Costa Ballena.
ServicesIndexPage.heroLede: Each URL targets a different search intent. Pick a service to read scope, typical pricing and our in-shop process.

FaqPage.metaDesc: Clear answers on quotes, timelines, paint systems, insurance, and service across Uvita, Dominical, Ojochal, and Bahía Ballena.
FaqPage.heroLede: Ranges, timelines, materials, insurance and coverage around Uvita. If your case is unusual, message us and we’ll walk through it.

items.q: What does a full respray typically cost?
items.a: A professional booth respray in Costa Rica often starts around ₡450,000 for a compact car in factory color and can go higher for SUVs, color changes or pearls. The real number comes from photos, inspection and actual panel scope — never a guess.

items.q: How long does full paint or structural frame work take?
items.a: Full paint: commonly 7–14 business days depending on prep, color and weather. Structural work: 5–20 days depending on damage. We give a range after diagnosis — we don’t promise dates by eyeball.

items.q: How do photo quotes over WhatsApp work?
items.a: Send one wide shot, a close-up of the damage and, if you can, year/make/model. We check framing and may ask for another angle. That gives a clear estimate. If we need to see structure or alignment, the next step is an in-shop inspection.

items.q: What vehicle brands and classes do you work on?
items.a: Any brand: sedans, pickups, SUVs and light commercial vehicles — within what our team and equipment can stand behind. If it’s outside our scope, we say so up front instead of taking the job anyway.
We don’t collect payment directly from insurers. We do prepare labour, materials and photo documentation for your adjuster or agent. You run the claim; we do the repair properly and document it.

items.q: What makes your shop different from a “dealer-authorised” paint-only shop?
items.a: The difference is process: controlled booth, cure cycles, branded systems and structural measurement when it matters. Many shops “spray paint”; we measure, pull structure when needed, then paint — with a written warranty and no rushing.

items.q: Which paint brands and chemicals do you use — and why does it matter?
items.a: Roberlo, BESA, 3M and VICCO in the right stack for substrate and color. The full system (primer, base, clear, mixing) drives durability, color match and warranty work. No random cans off the shelf.

items.q: How do I book, and where are you in Uvita?
items.a: Coordinate by WhatsApp. Posted hours (Mon–Sat, 8–5) are the frame; we confirm day-to-day based on load and job type. We’re in Uvita, Puntarenas, Costa Ballena — the public pin is on Google Maps; we share street-level detail when we lock a drop-off.

items.q: Which areas do you serve — Dominical, Ojochal, Uvita?
items.a: Drivers and residents across the South Zone of Puntarenas, including Uvita, Dominical, Ojochal, Bahía Ballena and nearby towns, with realistic timing. If distance is borderline, we discuss it before booking.

Usually cash and SINPE transfer. For longer jobs we agree milestone deposits in writing before we start. If you can’t make a confirmed drop-off, tell us early: rescheduling is normal; if you no-show a reserved bay, the next available slot will reflect the rules we agree on when we confirm.

items.q: What should I bring on drop-off or pick-up day?
items.a: Vehicle paperwork, full key set and, if relevant, parts or accessories tied to the repair. At delivery we do a visual check under light, a signed checklist, and aftercare notes (e.g. don’t stack cargo on fresh clear) — explained in person or in a clear message.

items.q: How does the warranty work — and what is never covered?
items.a: Each job carries a written warranty on our workmanship (prep, correction, application) per the agreed scope. It doesn’t cover new impacts, third-party rework on top of ours, harsh chemicals splashed on upholstery, or abuse. Exact limits also live on the warranty page and your work order; if something qualifies, we document it and fix it under the agreed warranty terms.

GuaranteePage.metaDesc: Every job leaves with a signed written warranty. If something fails due to a defect in our workmanship, we fix it at no charge within the agreed warranty period.
GuaranteePage.claimTitle: How to make a valid warranty claim
